skill.name: py-pydantic-patterns
skill.description: Practical Pydantic v2 patterns for validators, model configuration, field constraints, unions, custom types, serialization, and v1 migration.
skill.summary: Apply current Pydantic v2 patterns for reliable validation and serialization.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but review found they are false positives from Markdown code fences, inline Pydantic method names, and documentation text. The only real shell commands are local grep examples for finding deprecated v1 patterns, with no network access, secret access, or prompt injection evidence.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks and Pydantic Examples
low_findings.description: The external command detections are from Markdown code fences, inline method names, and Python examples. They document Pydantic v2 usage and do not execute commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are inside Markdown documentation or Python code examples. No executable skill script, dynamic command execution, or user-controlled shell invocation is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match ordinary documentation words such as description, deserializes, and Describe. No cryptographic function or algorithm use is shown.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context contains field descriptions and a Pydantic deserialization explanation. There is no hash, cipher, password storage, or cryptographic API call.

low_findings.title: False Positive: System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts match validation terms in sample models and an error table. They do not gather host, account, process, or network information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are validation examples and troubleshooting text. No system inspection command or API is present.

low_findings.title: Benign Local Search Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill includes grep commands to help developers find deprecated Pydantic v1 patterns in local Python files. These commands do not access secrets or remote services.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are static grep examples scoped to local source files. They are documented detection commands, not hidden automation executed by the skill.

content.value_statement: Pydantic v1 patterns can cause validation, serialization, and API response bugs in modern Python projects. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code concise Pydantic v2 patterns for safer model design.
actual_capabilities: Shows v1 to v2 API replacements for common Pydantic patterns.
Explains field validators, model validators, and validation order.
Demonstrates model configuration with ConfigDict and ORM object support.
Provides examples for field constraints, custom types, and discriminated unions.
Covers serialization controls, computed fields, and schema inheritance.
Includes grep commands for finding deprecated v1 patterns in Python files.
limitations: Does not execute tests or validate a project automatically.
Does not cover every Pydantic plugin or third-party integration.
Examples are general patterns and may need project-specific adaptation.
Requires the user to confirm behavior against the installed Pydantic version.

use_cases.title: Migrate Existing Schemas
use_cases.description: Replace deprecated Pydantic v1 decorators, config classes, and serialization calls with v2 patterns.

use_cases.title: Design API Request Models
use_cases.description: Create request and response models with strict validation, clear field constraints, and predictable serialization.

use_cases.title: Review Validation Logic
use_cases.description: Check model validators, custom types, and discriminated unions for maintainable Pydantic v2 usage.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Simple Model
prompt_templates.prompt: Use py-pydantic-patterns to create a Pydantic v2 model for this payload. Include field constraints and a short explanation.

prompt_templates.title: Convert v1 Syntax
prompt_templates.prompt: Use py-pydantic-patterns to update this Pydantic v1 model to v2. Explain each changed decorator, config option, and serialization method.

prompt_templates.title: Improve API Validation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use py-pydantic-patterns to review these API schemas. Add validators, ConfigDict settings, and serialization controls where appropriate.

prompt_templates.title: Model Complex Domain Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Use py-pydantic-patterns to design Pydantic v2 models for polymorphic records, custom normalized types, and read or write schema inheritance.

output_examples.input: Migrate a user schema that uses class Config, orm_mode, @validator, and dict().
output_examples.output: A v2 migration plan that replaces Config with ConfigDict, orm_mode with from_attributes, @validator with field_validator, and dict() with model_dump().

output_examples.input: Design models for a survey API with text, choice, and rating questions.
output_examples.output: A discriminated union model plan using a shared type field, separate question models, and an assessment container model.

output_examples.input: Review serialization for user responses that include UUID and datetime fields.
output_examples.output: A serialization checklist covering field serializers, computed fields, include and exclude options, alias handling, and JSON output.

best_practices: Use Pydantic v2 names such as model_validate, model_dump, field_validator, and ConfigDict.
Keep validators focused, typed, and explicit about before or after validation behavior.
Set model configuration deliberately for extra fields, ORM objects, defaults, and string normalization.
anti_patterns: Do not mix Pydantic v1 decorators and config classes into new v2 models.
Do not hide business rules in broad validators when field constraints can express them clearly.
Do not serialize API responses with ad hoc dictionaries when model_dump options can define the contract.

faq.question: What version of Pydantic does this skill target?
faq.answer: It targets Pydantic v2 patterns and highlights common replacements for v1 APIs.

faq.question: Can it migrate an entire repository automatically?
faq.answer: No. It provides patterns and detection commands, but code changes still require review and testing.

faq.question: Does it work with FastAPI projects?
faq.answer: Yes. The model patterns apply well to FastAPI request models, response models, and OpenAPI field metadata.

faq.question: Does it include serialization guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers model_dump, model_dump_json, field serializers, computed fields, include, exclude, and aliases.

faq.question: Can it help with ORM objects?
faq.answer: Yes. It shows ConfigDict with from_attributes for validating data from ORM-style objects.

faq.question: Is this a security scanning skill?
faq.answer: No. It is a coding reference for Pydantic v2 validation and serialization patterns.
